schema_version: v0 — migrates when Q6 (intention envelope) lands
owner: akiendelllinux_cc_0 (CC) — CC gates writes; the design is Akien-ratified (signature gate, 2026-07-21 design conversation: surfaces model, data/render split, artifact-owner spinoff, 'i think web server is /sorted').
intention: A thin web presentation surface (Law 7) over the running system: the devices on the heartbeat across the top, and the selected device's ACTIVE page below — a stack of standard PANES (STATUS / INTERACTION / SETTINGS / LOGGING; names are placeholders, final labels must read to an averageish human). The panes are STANDARD MACHINERY in BaseShim, not in each device and not in the web server: a device fills a pane by providing a handler, and declares WHICH panes it offers (STATUS + SETTINGS are the guaranteed floor via Form v0 #2; INTERACTION/LOGGING/future are declared, and a device may declare more than one of a kind — the librarian's chat-page + tool-page fall out as two declared interaction surfaces, not a special case). A pane with no handler renders empty/absent. The web server itself is a dumb HTTP<->bus bridge + frame renderer; it owns NO device state.
why: Telos 2 — the librarian needs a web interface, and 'a web interface falls out of the chatbot' only if the surface exists first; this is that surface, on the spine before the librarian. It is the Form v0 #2 introspection surface (MAP.md:157) MADE CONCRETE — the SAME protocol the tester probes and the web UI renders, so observability is not a separate build (STATUS = intention+why+state; SETTINGS = settings; INTERACTION = the reserved `other` channel). Reported-vs-filed intention on the STATUS pane is a live drift detector (Law 3). Building it as a presentation surface (Law 7: may collapse an error into a coherent shape; never a record of truth) keeps the intelligence in the shims and the web server trivial (minimalism; one owner, no state).
traces_to: Telos 2 (Cairn has a USER — the librarian's web interface rests on this surface)
Form v0 #2 (MAP.md:157 — the uniform introspection surface; one protocol for tester + web UI)
Law 7 (a presentation surface may collapse an error into a coherent shape; a record of truth never may)
Law 6 (the web server owns no state — content is each device's, reached through its gate; a kill travels the bus to the OWNER, the shim, which runs it — the web server only describes the command)
Law 4 (gates not supervisors; the circuit breaker halts by physics — cgroup-kill — not by asking a runaway to stop)
build order: web server, after inference domain (MAP.md:888)
state: code-seam@v1: THINKME -> TICKETME -> [BUILDME] -> PROVEME -> LEARNME -> PROVED   (cursor at BUILDME: design wrapped + ratified + decomposed. Children a/b/c are buildable now; d/e are deferred, each waiting on another node. Not yet built.)
gates: proof gate (tester) — the tester runs each child's proof and emits a VALIDATION, verdict+seal from the same hand. THIS SESSION'S LESSON, made a gate: the VALIDATION must be PERSISTED via db_domain (store.persist_validation) and grep back — a produced-but-unpersisted validation did NOT happen (the exact miss that bit inference_domain, 2026-07-21). Durability gate (code-seam): not done until proven, VALIDATION persisted, AND committed (git). Kicks back on red proof -> design/build at the point of creation (CP2).
falsifier: the surface is honest and thin: a device's ACTIVE page shows exactly the panes it DECLARES (no more, no less); STATUS shows reported-vs-filed intention and flags drift when they differ; the web server holds NO device state of its own (everything rendered is pulled live from the device's shim over the bus, from disk intentions, or from db_domain reads). Wrong-shape signals: (1) if presentation logic migrates INTO the shims (shims emitting HTML instead of surface DATA), the tester can no longer probe the same protocol — the data/render split has failed; (2) if the web server accretes its own state/cache of device internals, it has stopped being a presentation surface and become a second record of truth (Law 7 breach); (3) if the standard panes stop being standard (each device re-implementing its own STATUS), BaseShim was the wrong home.
horizon: first real render — when the web server shows at least two live devices' ACTIVE pages pulled over the bus, STATUS drift-detection works, and the LOGGING pane reads real emission. Re-check then: is BaseShim the right home for the standard machinery, or do too many devices override it? And revisit the data/render split the first time an INTERACTION surface needs a rich device-provided view (the librarian's tool page) — standard panes = data + generic render, interaction panes = may bring their own template.
children: (BUILT + PROVEN + PERSISTED 2026-07-22, cairn) a — the standard surface machinery in BaseShim: a device DECLARES its panes and provides handlers; BaseShim assembles the ACTIVE payload as structured DATA (extending introspect(): intention/state/settings/other -> the pane set). Surfaces are a declared LIST, not a fixed enum; STATUS+SETTINGS are the floor, the rest declared, multiples allowed. LANDED: BaseDevice.declared_panes() (default [], the offered extras with handlers) + BaseShim.active_page() (the standard assembly — STATUS+SETTINGS projected from introspect(), declared panes appended in order, multiples allowed, an unwired or raising handler renders ABSENT-with-reason not fatal (CP2/Law 7), the whole page json-round-trips so it is DATA not HTML). Proof cairn/base/proofs/test_active_page.py (5 teeth), green under the tester, VALIDATION persisted beside-code at cairn/base/validations/test_active_page.json. FILED EDGE: STATUS carries only the REPORTED intention; the reported-vs-filed DRIFT comparison is child b's render-time job (it reads disk intentions), not the shim's.

(buildable) b — the web_server device: a standing daemon (launched via launchers/, instance at ~/.cairn/devices/web_server/0/), owning its listening PORT (Law 6). A dumb HTTP<->bus bridge: browser <-> HTTP <-> web_server <-> bus <-> each device's shim. Renders the standard panes' DATA to HTML (interaction panes may bring their own view). v0: bind LOCALHOST, single operator (Akien) — it exposes device internals and (later) a kill switch. Python stdlib http.server; no framework unless a real need pulls one.

(buildable) c — ground_loop contracted to publish the live heartbeat ROSTER (the devices it beats to) at all times; that roster IS the nav across the top. Extends the heartbeat's existing knowledge of its subscribers — no new owner.

(deferred, waits on the artifact-owner device) d — the JOURNEY pane: follow one journey_id across ALL devices (the cross-device trace only Cairn's architecture gives). Owned by the artifact-owner (the new device that owns tickets+callbacks+journals — its own /intent, cast separately). This cashes out the map's Events-vs-Journeys split: LOGGING pane = per-device Events (cache-class), journey pane = cross-device Journeys (truth-class, owned centrally). A device may still declare its OWN ticket view (its slice) via the same machinery.

(deferred, waits on the cgroup-kill sudo script) e — the SETTINGS pane's CIRCUIT BREAKER: the web server presents the button; the halt travels the bus to the device's shim, which kills the device's cgroup subtree (cgroup v2 cgroup.kill — inescapable by forking/re-parenting, the likely cause of past failed kills) via a narrow least-privilege sudo script (sudo-relay pattern). Presentation describes; the owner runs; the kill is physics (Law 4).
waits_on: nothing for the v0 build (children a/b/c). Children d and e wait on other nodes (the artifact-owner device; the cgroup-kill sudo script) and land as grafts when those exist.
provenance: cast 2026-07-21 via /sorted at the close of the web_server design conversation, after a session that (1) built inference_domain WITHOUT a ticket and without persisting its validation — the miss this ticket's gate now guards against — and (2) converged the surfaces-in-the-shim model with Akien. The design resolved the map's open Q4 (introspection protocol: browser<->HTTP<->web_server<->bus<->shim). Spun off a sibling node: the ARTIFACT-OWNER device (owns proven-space artifacts — tickets, callbacks, journals — the emit-chokepoint + method-registry given a home; ledger + transition physics, NEVER execution, or it becomes the ground_loop-executor goof). Ratified by Akien: 'i think web server is /sorted'.
